context: Evaluates a Vue 3 composable that manages light/dark theme state. The composable must read the initial theme from localStorage, fall back to the system's prefers-color-scheme preference, apply the theme via a data attribute on the document root, and expose reactive helpers for querying and toggling the theme. Persistence and system integration are both required.

checklist.name: localStorage persistence
checklist.description: Saves the selected theme to `localStorage` under the key `"theme"` when `setTheme` is called, and reads from this key during initialization to restore a previously chosen theme.

checklist.name: System preference fallback
checklist.description: When no theme is stored in `localStorage`, reads the `prefers-color-scheme` media query to determine the initial theme (`"dark"` if the query matches, `"light"` otherwise).

checklist.name: data-coreui-theme attribute
checklist.description: Applies the active theme by setting `document.documentElement.setAttribute("data-coreui-theme", theme)` so CoreUI components render in the correct theme.

checklist.name: toggleTheme function
checklist.description: Implements `toggleTheme()` that switches from `"dark"` to `"light"` or from `"light"` to `"dark"` and calls `setTheme` with the new value.

checklist.name: isDark and isLight helpers
checklist.description: Provides `isDark()` returning `true` when `theme.value === "dark"` and `isLight()` returning `true` when `theme.value === "light"`, using the reactive `theme` ref.
